3.happening: Reading the length of the string into n.
3.why: The pointers need to know the last valid index before they can expand.
3.invariant: n stays fixed for the whole search.

4.happening: Setting start to 0 — the best palindrome begins as just the first character.
4.why: We need a running best to compare every new palindrome against.
4.invariant: The span from start to end is always a real palindrome.

5.happening: Setting end to 0 — so far the best palindrome is a single character.
5.why: A one-character substring is always a palindrome, so it is a safe starting best.
5.invariant: start and end always describe the best palindrome found so far.

6.happening: Choosing index {center} as the next palindrome center.
6.why: Every palindrome has a center, so trying all centers is guaranteed to find the longest.
6.invariant: Centers already tried cannot hide a longer palindrome.

7.happening: First pass for this center: offset 0 tests an odd-length palindrome.
7.why: An odd-length palindrome has a single middle character.
7.invariant: Both pointers will start on the same cell.

7.happening: Second pass: offset 1 tests an even-length palindrome centered in the gap.
7.why: An even-length palindrome has its center between two characters.
7.invariant: The right pointer will start one cell past the left.

8.happening: Placing the left pointer on the center at index {center}.
8.why: Both pointers begin at the center and grow outward together.
8.invariant: left never moves right during a single expansion.

9.happening: Placing the right pointer at index {center + offset}.
9.why: For an odd center it equals the center; for an even center it is the next cell.
9.invariant: left and right stay symmetric around the center.

10.happening: Testing the pointers at indices {left} and {right}: still in bounds and matching?
10.why: While the characters match, the substring between the pointers is a palindrome that can still grow.
10.invariant: Everything strictly between left and right is already a confirmed palindrome.

11.happening: Match confirmed — moving the left pointer outward from index {left}.
11.why: Expanding left grows the palindrome by one character on the left side.
11.invariant: The characters just inside the pointers are equal in pairs.

12.happening: Match confirmed — moving the right pointer outward from index {right}.
12.why: Expanding right grows the palindrome by one character on the right side.
12.invariant: left and right remain symmetric about the center.

13.happening: This palindrome (length {right - left - 1}) beats the best so far (length {end - start + 1}) — record it.
13.why: A longer palindrome should replace the current champion.
13.invariant: start and end are about to point at the new best.

13.happening: This palindrome (length {right - left - 1}) is not longer than the best so far (length {end - start + 1}) — keep the current champion.
13.why: The recorded best already covers at least as many characters.
13.invariant: start and end keep pointing at the previous best.

14.happening: New best — recording the start index as {left + 1}.
14.why: The palindrome sits just inside where the left pointer stopped.
14.invariant: start now points at the first character of the best palindrome.

15.why: The right pointer overshot by one, so the palindrome ends one cell inside it.
15.invariant: The span from start to end is the longest palindrome found so far.

16.happening: Slicing out the champion palindrome from index {start} to {end}.
16.why: start and end mark the best palindrome discovered across every center.
16.invariant: No untried center could contain a longer palindrome.

17.why: Every center has been expanded, so the recorded best is the final answer.

init.happening: Setting up the length and the running best palindrome.
init.why: We need bounds and a best-so-far before expanding.
init.invariant: The span from start to end is always a valid palindrome.

loop.happening: Iterating through centers and expansion passes.
loop.why: Trying every center guarantees the longest palindrome is found.
loop.invariant: The best palindrome so far is remembered in start and end.

move.happening: Positioning or advancing the expansion pointers.
move.why: The pointers grow the palindrome outward from its center.
move.invariant: left and right stay symmetric about the center.

check.happening: Comparing the new palindrome's length to the best.
check.why: Only a longer palindrome should replace the champion.
check.invariant: start and end always hold the longest palindrome seen.

update.why: A longer palindrome was found and must be remembered.
update.invariant: start and end describe the current best.

return.invariant: The answer is the best palindrome across all centers.
